name: Fé

old_icelandic.original: Fé er frænda róg ok flæðar viti ok grafseiðs gata.
old_icelandic.translation: Wealth is source of discord among kin and fire of the sea and path of the serpent.

old_norwegian.original: Fé vældr frænda róge; føðesk ulfr í skóge.
old_norwegian.translation: Wealth causes friction among kinsmen; the wolf thrives in the woods.

practical_application: Managing mobile capital, navigating social friction, and communal protection.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Carve ᚠ into stone or wood while naming the debt or resource.
Speak the Norwegian stanza to recognize the 'wolf' (risk) in hoarding.

source_reference: Various Viking Age Runestones (e.g., Jelling Stones)

name: Úr

old_icelandic.original: Úr er skýja grátr ok skára þverrir ok hirðis hatr.
old_icelandic.translation: Rain is lamentation of the clouds and destroyer of the hay-harvest.

old_norwegian.original: Úr er af illu jarne; opt løypr ræinn á hjarne.
old_norwegian.translation: Dross comes from bad iron; the reindeer often races over the frozen snow.

practical_application: Endurance through purification and hard trials.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Speak the Icelandic stanza when seeking to endure a difficult 'storm' or trial.

name: Þurs

old_icelandic.original: Þurs er kvenna kvöl ok kletta búi ok varðrúnar verr.
old_icelandic.translation: Giant is torture of women and cliff-dweller.

old_norwegian.original: Þurs vældr kvinna kvillu; kátr værðr fár af illu.
old_norwegian.translation: Giant causes anguish to women; misfortune makes few men cheerful.

practical_application: Establishing a wall of protection against chaotic forces.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Invoke the 'Thorn-Wall' to protect a space from external intrusion.

name: Óss

old_icelandic.original: Óss er aldingautr ok ásgarðs jöfurr, ok valhallar vísi.
old_icelandic.translation: God is the aged Gautr and prince of Asgard and lord of Valhalla.

old_norwegian.original: Óss er flæstra færða fǫr; en skalpr er sværða.
old_norwegian.translation: Estuary is the way of most journeys; but a scabbard is of swords.

practical_application: Sacred gateway for commerce and journeys; inspiration and speech.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Speak the name to hallow the voice before a legal or sacred speech.

name: Reið

old_icelandic.original: Reið er sitjandi sæla ok snörp ferð ok jórs erfiði.
old_icelandic.translation: Riding is joy of the horsemen and speedy journey.

old_norwegian.original: Ræið kveða rossom væsta; Reginn sló sværðet bæzta.
old_norwegian.translation: Riding is said to be the worst thing for horses.

practical_application: Maintaining physical order on the long road.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Establishing a cadence for a journey or a multi-day project.

name: Kaun

old_icelandic.original: Kaun er barna böl ok bardaga för ok holds fúa hús.
old_icelandic.translation: Ulcer is disease fatal to children and abode of mortification.

old_norwegian.original: Kaun er barna bǫlvan; bǫl gørver nán fǫlvan.
old_norwegian.translation: Ulcer is fatal to children; death makes a corpse pale.

practical_application: Identifying and warding off internal rot and decay.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Naming a 'sickness' or systemic problem to expose it to the light.

name: Hagall

old_icelandic.original: Hagall er kaldakorn ok krapadrífa ok snáka sótt.
old_icelandic.translation: Hail is cold grain and shower of sleet.

old_norwegian.original: Hagall er kaldastr korna; Kristr skóp hæimenn forna.
old_norwegian.translation: Hail is the coldest of grain; Christ created the world of old.

practical_application: Forces of nature that mandate taking shelter.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Establishing a sanctuary to endure an unavoidable external crisis.

name: Nauð

old_icelandic.original: Nauð er þýjar þrá ok þungr kostr ok váshöm drjúg.
old_icelandic.translation: Need is grief of the bond-maid and state of oppression.

old_norwegian.original: Nauðr gerer næppa koste; nøktan kælr í froste.
old_norwegian.translation: Constraint gives scant choice; a naked man is chilled.

practical_application: Fortifying resolve during trial and scarcity.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Naming the specific constraint to find the 'scant choice' required for survival.

name: Íss

old_icelandic.original: Íss er árbörkr ok unnar þak ok feigra fár.
old_icelandic.translation: Ice is bark of rivers and roof of the wave.

old_norwegian.original: Ís kǫllum brú bræiða; blindan þarf at læiða.
old_norwegian.translation: Ice we call the broad bridge; the blind man must be led.

practical_application: Stillness and preservation; the roof of the wave.

ceremony_roadmap.action: A period of complete silence to 'freeze' a situation or preserve an intent.

name: Ár

old_icelandic.original: Ár er gumna góði ok gott sumar ok algróinn akr.
old_icelandic.translation: Plenty is boon to men and a thriving field.

old_norwegian.original: Ár er gumna góðe; get ek at ǫrr var Fróðe.
old_norwegian.translation: Plenty is a boon to men; I say that Frodi was generous.

practical_application: Peaceful abundance and reward of steady growth.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Acknowledging the completion of a growth cycle and the 'peace' of success.

name: Sól

old_icelandic.original: Sól er skýja skjöldr ok skínandi röðull ok ísa aldrtregi.
old_icelandic.translation: Sun is shield of the clouds and destroyer of ice.

old_norwegian.original: Sól er landa ljóme; lúti ek helgum dóme.
old_norwegian.translation: Sun is the light of the world; I bow to the divine decree.

practical_application: Clarity for seafarers and the heat that breaks stasis.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Seeking the 'ray' of the sun to reveal the path forward.

name: Týr

old_icelandic.original: Týr er einhendr áss ok ulfs leifar ok hofa hilmir.
old_icelandic.translation: Tyr is one-handed god and prince of temples.

old_norwegian.original: Týr er æinendr ása; opt værðr smiðr blása.
old_norwegian.translation: Tyr is a one-handed god; often has the smith to blow.

practical_application: Sacrifice for the community and unwavering duty.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Naming the specific cost or effort being offered for the success of the group.

name: Bjarkan

old_icelandic.original: Bjarkan er laufgat lim ok lítit tré ok ungsamligr viðr.
old_icelandic.translation: Birch is leafy twig and fresh young shrub.

old_norwegian.original: Bjarkan er laufgrønstr líma; Loki bar flærða tíma.
old_norwegian.translation: Birch has the greenest leaves; Loki was fortunate in deceit.

practical_application: Renewal and the emergence of something new; concealment.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Planting an intent or naming a new project to 'emerge'.

name: Maðr

old_icelandic.original: Maðr er manns gaman ok moldar auki ok skipa skreytir.
old_icelandic.translation: Man is delight of man and augmentation of the earth.

old_norwegian.original: Maðr er moldar auki; mikil er græip á hauki.
old_norwegian.translation: Man is an augmentation of the dust; great is the claw of the hawk.

practical_application: The delight and mortality of the human condition.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Naming a kinsman or partner as the 'delight' of the current endeavor.

name: Lögr

old_icelandic.original: Lögr er vellanda vatn ok víðr ketill ok glömmungr grund.
old_icelandic.translation: Water is eddying stream and fishes' field.

old_norwegian.original: Lǫgr er, fællr ór fjalle foss; en gull ero nosser.
old_norwegian.translation: A waterfall is a River which falls from a mountain-side.

practical_application: Intuition and the physical flow of commerce and life.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Pouring a libation into a bowl to simulate the 'eddying stream'.

name: Ýr

old_icelandic.original: Ýr er bendr bogi ok brotgjarnt járn ok fífu fárbauti.
old_icelandic.translation: Yew is a bent bow and battle-help.

old_norwegian.original: Ýr er vetrgrønstr viða; vænt er, er brennr, at sviða.
old_norwegian.translation: Yew is the greenest of trees in winter.

practical_application: The endurance of the bent bow and battle-readiness.

ceremony_roadmap.action: Establishing the 'bent bow' (tension and focus) before an ordeal.
